confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_36f69b06-55a7-4f28-9640-f2d3daabc8f3 read back as archived

proposed_factorization: Site config declares which external regimes the Site recognizes, their binding scope, and when they apply.
Doctrine/KG/KB stores the actual interpretation, mappings, obligations, and process implications.
Operation configs opt into or inherit applicable doctrine imports such as QMSR, ISO 13485, CAPA, complaints, design-development, supplier controls, records, and validation.
Task lifecycle projects active doctrine imports into evidence requirements, review/approval roles, traceability, and closure gates.
Config should preserve source citation, authority, version/effective date, and binding posture, but not attempt to encode the whole external doctrine as prose.
requested_execution: Add a Site config schema concept/key such as doctrine_imports or governing_regimes.
Define fields for id, kind, authority, citation/source, effective date/version, binding scope, applicability predicates, and inheritance behavior.
Document the distinction between importing a regime and interpreting it.
Add a small example showing FDA QMSR admitted at Site level and inherited by a CAPA Operation.
payload.summary: Narada Site config should be able to declare external doctrine/regulatory regimes recognized by the Site, such as FDA QMSR / 21 CFR Part 820, without embedding the doctrine itself in config. The config should declare admission, scope, binding posture, and inheritance rules; doctrine/KG/KB should hold interpretation and mappings; Operation and task machinery should project those imports into concrete evidence, review, approval, and traceability gates.
payload.title: Add Site config support for governed doctrine imports and external constraint regimes

promotion.note: Envelope archived; no target-zone mutation was performed.
